---
title: "Fun Docs"
---

From time to time I come across fun or interesting archival documents. I've
started collecting them here.

## Superpowers: Telekinesis

A CIA-led experiment reporting that one subject displayed paranormal abilities of
telekinesis — in particular, that he could heat objects by touching them.

<!-- This PDF currently lives on the old Wix site. To keep it after the move,
     save it into files/fun-docs/ and change the link to a local path. -->
[Read the document](https://www.michaelfjoseph.com/_files/ugd/e0f145_d5624109e08442a19ae8587d6cef8008.pdf)

## Superpowers: Remote Viewing

A review by the Federation of American Scientists of programs testing whether
humans can perceive distant objects — a little like Professor X in *X-Men*. The
report suggests some subjects' estimates were accurate beyond what chance would
predict.

[irp.fas.org/program/collect/air1995.pdf](https://irp.fas.org/program/collect/air1995.pdf)

## UFOs

A comprehensive review of classified CIA programs on UFOs. Interestingly, the
Soviets and the PRC ran dedicated UFO programs of their own — and the CIA worked
hard to spy on them.

[cia.gov/readingroom/docs/DOC_0000838057.pdf](https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/DOC_0000838057.pdf)

## $22 Million Underpants

This year, the IARPA program
["Smart e-Pants"](https://www.iarpa.gov/research-programs/smart-epants) awarded
$22 million to build underwear.
